name: umnet-scripts

metadata.name: umnet-scripts
metadata.summary: Python tools for interacting with UMnet's network tools

documentation:
# umnet-scripts
A set of python modules/classes for interacting with UMnet tools.

This package is hosted on pypi - you can install it with `pip install umnet-scripts` and use it in your own code.

# Database Helper Classes
As of Dec 2021 this repo defines db wrapper classes for Equipdb, Netinfo, and Netdisco.
To use these classes you need to set specific environment variables with the credentials
for each db respectively:
* Netinfo: `NETINFO_USERNAME`, `NETINFO_PASSSWORD`
* Netdisco: `NETDISCO_DB_USER`, `NETDISCO_DB_PASSWORD`
* Equipdb: `EQUIP_DB_USER`, `EQUIP_DB_PASSWORD`

Queries for specific things are added as they're needed. As of Dec 2021 there are only a few methods defined for netdisco and equipdb.
Each class inherits a super basic sql query builder method as well as an 'execute' method from a base class (see `umnetdb.py`).

# Rancid Helper Class
The rancid helper class currently parses the router.db files in `/home/rancid/` as well as the `/home/rancid/Topology`
into a list of `Device` objects with rancid-related data as attributes, particularly the following:
* `rancid_name`: The name of the device as rancid knows it (the name of the config file). IP address for AL, DNS name for everything else.
* `rancid_type`: The rancid perl script that is used to back up the device (eg `juniper` or `Cat3750`)
* `rancid_role`: This is the folder the device config lives in, eg `accesslayer` or `core`. Maps 1:1 to the equipdb device `type`.
* `model`: Device model as populated in equipdb, eg 'EX2200-24T-4G'
* `cfg_file`: Full path to the config file, eg `/home/rancid/accesslayer/10.233.0.10`
* `status`: Up or down per the equipdb `offline` flag (offline is down).
* `neighbors`: A dict of the device's neighbors as reported by TopologyWalker.

There are also two attributes that look like attributes but are actually 'property' functions: `hostname` and `ip` that return the device's hostname
or IP respectively. If you call 'hostname' for a device whose rancid name is an IP, a dns reverse lookup is done. Similarly,
if you call 'ip' on a device whose rancid name is a DNS name, a forward dns lookup is done.

Note that if you're only focusing on one rancid config folder (eg datacenter, accesslayer, etc),
to speed your code up you can limit which folders are processed when you instantiate the Rancid class.
Note that if you want to get devices by dl_zone you *must* include the distlayer folder at minimum.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetlb.UMnetLB.__init__
documentation: Validate lb hostname before attempting to connect

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetlb.UMnetLB.get_pool_member_state
documentation:
Returns a list of pool members and their status. If a pool is provided,
only that pool is returned. If a context is provided, only pools in that
context are returned.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetlb.UMnetLB.get_vip_status
documentation:
Queries the F5 for VIP status. If you provide the vip and the partition,
only that VIP is requested. Otherwise all VIPs from all partitions are retrieved
and filtered based on input.

Output for each vip includes the name, ip:port, 
    partition, pool, status, and reason status

classes.name: umnet_scripts.umnetdb.UMnetdb

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetdb.UMnetdb.__init__
documentation:
DB initialization is unique enough across our various dbs that this is implemented
in the child classes (at least for now).

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetdb.UMnetdb._build_select
documentation:
Generic 'select' query string builder built from standard query components as input.
The user is required to generate substrings for the more complex inputs
(eg joins, where statements), this function just puts all the components
together in the right order with appropriately-added newlines (for debugging purposes)
and returns the result.

:select: a list of columns to select
  ex: ["nip.mac", "nip.ip", "n.switch", "n.port"]
:table: a string representing a table (or comma-separated tables, with our without aliases)
  ex: "node_ip nip"
:joins: a list of strings representing join statements. Include the actual 'join' part!
  ex: ["join node n on nip.mac = n.mac", "join device d on d.ip = n.switch"]
:where: A list of 'where' statements without the 'where'. The list of statements are
  "anded". If you need "or", embed it in one of your list items
  ex: ["node_ip.ip = '1.2.3.4'", "node.switch = '10.233.0.5'"]
:order_by: A string representing a column name (or names) to order by
:group_by: A string representing a column name (or names) to group by
:limit: An integer

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetdb.UMnetdb._execute
documentation: Generic sqlalchemy "execute this sql command and give me all the results"
This class wraps helpful netinfo db queries in python.

functions.name: umnet_scripts.utils.interface_sort
documentation:
Meant to be passed as a sort function for a list of interface names, or a list
of dictionaries representing interface data

If you're dealing with a list of dictionaries, the port name must be keyed as "port"
or "name". If it's not, the function will throw an error.

It is assumed that all ports have at least a mod/port component (eg GiX/Y), and it will also
handle a slot/mod/number (eg GiX/Y/Z). "X" is weighted by 1000, "Y" by 100, and Z by 1.

functions.name: umnet_scripts.utils.get_env_vars
documentation:
Pull list of variables from the environment,
store in dict

functions.name: umnet_scripts.utils.is_mac_address
documentation:
Validates the input string as a mac address. Valid formats are
XX:XX:XX:XX:XX:XX, XX-XX-XX-XX-XX-XX, XXXX.XXXX.XXXX
where 'X' is a hexadecimal digit (upper or lowercase).

functions.name: umnet_scripts.utils.forward_resolve
documentation: Looks for an A record for an inputted DNS name

functions.name: umnet_scripts.utils.reverse_resolve
documentation: looks for a PTR record for an inputted IPv4 address

functions.name: umnet_scripts.utils.resolve_name_or_ip
documentation:
Resolves a DNS name or an IPv4 address.
Returns {'name':<hostname>, 'ip':<ip>}
If a lookup didn't get an answer, returns None
as that value

functions.name: umnet_scripts.utils.expand_interface
documentation:
Expands a cisco interface to its full name.
If no match is found, the string is returned
unchanged

functions.name: umnet_scripts.utils.get_ncs_interface_name
documentation:
Shortens a cisco interface from its full name
to an abbreviation. Also removes '.0' from juniper interfaces

methods.name: umnet_scripts.rancid.Device.__iter__
documentation: Defined so you can use dict() on a device object.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.rancid.Device.hostname
documentation:
The hostname of the device. A reverse DNS lookup is done if the
device's rancid name is an IP address.
If the reverse lookup fails for any reason, None is returned.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.rancid.Device.ip
documentation:
The IP address of the device. A DNS lookup is done if the
device's rancid name is a DNS name. If the lookup fails then
'None' is returned.

classes.name: umnet_scripts.rancid.Rancid

methods.name: umnet_scripts.rancid.Rancid.get_file_lines
documentation:
Opens the specified file on the local or remote device,
depending on what was specified upon instantiation.
If the file doesn't exist an error is logged 
and an empty list is returned.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.rancid.Rancid._parse_rdb
documentation: Parses a router db file

methods.name: umnet_scripts.rancid.Rancid._set_device_neighbors
documentation:
Given a path to a ".ifTable" file on the local
filesystem, parses the file into a dict of
on interface name.

Also shortens/cleans up port names to match their
well-known abbreviations

methods.name: umnet_scripts.rancid.Rancid._set_device_lag_members
documentation:
Looks for a topologywalker ".AGG" file for the device
and parses the data into a dict mapping

methods.name: umnet_scripts.rancid.Rancid._parse_verstat
documentation:
Parses /home/rancid/VER/VERstat into a mapping of
devices to their software versions (only)

methods.name: umnet_scripts.rancid.Rancid.get_device
documentation: returns a device based on its rancid name

methods.name: umnet_scripts.rancid.Rancid.get_devices
documentation:
Look up a set of devices based on any set of device attributes.
If no arguments are provided, the full device list is returned.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.rancid.Rancid.get_dlzone
documentation:
Returns a list of devices in the same DL zone
based on neighbors.

A class that provides easy access to rancid data.

When you instantiate the class you can set the 'remote' argument
to specify whether you want the code to search the local filesystem,
or if you want it to search a remote server (default is wallace).

When using a remote connection, you must either provide the constructor
with a remote_user and remote_password, or you can let
remote_user default to "rancid" and set a RANCID_PASSWORD
environment variable on your local machine.

modules.name: umnet_scripts.googlesheet
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

methods.name: umnet_scripts.googlesheet.UMGoogleSheet._col_to_a1
documentation: Converts a column number to A1 notation.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.googlesheet.UMGoogleSheet.open_spreadsheet
documentation: Opens a spreadsheet by url or by key. Detects a url by looking for 'https'

methods.name: umnet_scripts.googlesheet.UMGoogleSheet.get_worksheet
documentation:
Gets a worksheet by name. If return as dict is set to true (default is yet),
will return the results of "get all records". Otherwise will return a worksheet object.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.googlesheet.UMGoogleSheet.get_worksheet_values
documentation:
numericise_ignore (list) – (optional) List of ints of indices of the
columns (starting at 1) to ignore numericising, special use of [‘all’]
to ignore numericising on all columns.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.googlesheet.UMGoogleSheet.open_and_get_worksheet
documentation: Opens a spreadsheet and gets a worksheet by name from that spreadsheet.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.googlesheet.UMGoogleSheet.open_and_get_worksheet_values
documentation:
numericise_ignore (list) – (optional) List of ints of indices of the
columns (starting at 1) to ignore numericising, special use of [‘all’]
to ignore numericising on all columns.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.googlesheet.UMGoogleSheet.find_row_in_worksheet
documentation:
Finds the first row on a specific worksheet where the search term
matches a cell in that row. If it is found, will either
return as a dict with header (row 1) as the keys, or as a simple list

methods.name: umnet_scripts.googlesheet.UMGoogleSheet.find_rows_in_worksheet
documentation:
Finds ALL rows on a specific worksheet where the search term
matches a cell in that row. If it is found, will either
return as a list of dicts with header (row 1) as the keys, or as a list of lists

methods.name: umnet_scripts.googlesheet.UMGoogleSheet.create_or_overwrite_worksheet
documentation:
Creates a new worksheet or overwrites an existing one
with the data provided.

If the data is a dictionary, the keys become a header for row 1
Otherwise data is assumed to be a list of lists of all the same
length (letting gspreads api validate that)

methods.name: umnet_scripts.googlesheet.UMGoogleSheet.diff_worksheets
documentation:
Compares the data on two worksheets. Rows that don't match are
highlighted on both sheets

methods.name: umnet_scripts.googlesheet.UMGoogleSheet.diff_worksheet_rows
documentation:
Compares the data on two worksheets but ignores row order - it is assumed that
the columns on both sheets are the same and we want those to match.

You must provide a column index that allows us to uniquely identify each row.
IMPORTANT: ** Column indexes must be provided as integers (not letters) and start at 1 **
That index is used to map rows on sheet 1 to rows on sheet 2. Once a mapping is found
that row on sheet 1 is compared to the same row on sheet 2 and vice versa.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.googlesheet.UMGoogleSheet._rows_compare
documentation:
Workhorse of the rows compare - compares ws1 to ws2 and updates the formats on ws1
where discrepancies are found

methods.name: umnet_scripts.googlesheet.UMGoogleSheet.clear_text_and_background_colors
documentation:
Clears the text color and background colors (sets text to black and background to white)
tbd: figure out how to clear all formatting. You'd think they'd have a way to

modules.name: umnet_scripts.cyberark
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetnso_restconf.UMnetnso._get_restconf
documentation: does a 'get' of the restconf data resource and returns the results

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetnso_restconf.UMnetnso._restconf_put
documentation:
Does a PUT and returns the HTTP status code.
Per RFC8040 4.5, a PUT creates or replaces a
node. The client provides the name of the node,
the path to the node, and the data tied to the node.

The server returns HTTP status code 201 if the node is created,
204 if the node is replaced. This function returns
the status code.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetnso_restconf.UMnetnso.put_device
documentation:
Creates or over-writes a device in NSO. Note that devices are keyed
on name, not IP.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetnso_restconf.UMnetnso.get_all_devices
documentation: Gets basic info related to devices in ncs

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetnso_restconf.UMnetnso.get_all_device_groups
documentation: Pulls a list of all the device groups in nso

classes.name: umnet_scripts.umnetdisco.Umnetdisco

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetdisco.Umnetdisco.host_arp
documentation:
Does an ARP query against the netdisco db for a single host. 
:host: A string representing a MAC address or an IPv4 address

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetdisco.Umnetdisco.arp_count
documentation:
Queries the host data in netdisco based on prefix and start/end time.
First any prefix greater than or equal to the inputted prefix is searched for
(in the 'device_ip' table).

Then the host table is queried.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetdisco.Umnetdisco.neighbors
documentation:
Queries the device_ip table in netdisco to get neighbors of a device.
If no device is specified, all neighbors are pulled.
The device input can be an IPv4 address or an FQDN.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetdisco.Umnetdisco.get_devices
documentation:
Queries netdisco for a list of devices.
Optionally, limit it by a list of prefixes

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetdisco.Umnetdisco.get_device
documentation:
Pulls the device name, ip, model, and description (where version info often is)
from the netinfo device table

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetdisco.Umnetdisco.get_port_and_host_data
documentation:
Pulls a list of all ports on the switch and all hosts seen on those ports.
You will see multiple rows for an interface where multiple hosts have been seen.

Returns a list of ports along with:
- port name
- port description
- admin status
- oper status
- description
- host mac
- host IP
- vlan the host was seen on
- date last mac was seen

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetdisco.Umnetdisco.get_poe_data
documentation: Pulls PoE admin, status, class, and power columns from device_port_pwer

This class wraps helpful netdisco db queries in python.
The API is lame.

classes.name: umnet_scripts.umnetequip.UMnetequip

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetequip.UMnetequip.get_devices_by_category
documentation:
Queries equip db for devices by category. You can also
specify if you only want active devices.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetequip.UMnetequip.get_device_type
documentation:
Queries equip db for a device by ip, returns the 'type' of the device.
By type we mean the UMNET_EQUIPMENT_TYPE: ACCESS LAYER, DISTRIBUTION LAYER, UMD, etc

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetequip.UMnetequip.get_devices_by_building_no
documentation:
Queries equipdb for devices by building no. You can provide a single
building number, or a list of numbers. You can specify if you want 'active only' devices
(based on the 'status' field, defalut false) or you can limit to a certain device type (default all).
You can also get the location info for each device via 'location_info' (default false)

This class wraps helpful equip db queries in python.
The API is lame.

classes.name: umnet_scripts.umnetinfo.UMnetinfo

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetinfo.UMnetinfo.get_network_by_name
documentation: Looks up a network by netname.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetinfo.UMnetinfo.get_network_by_ip
documentation:
Looks up a network based on an IPv4 or IPv6 address. Returns the netname,
vlan id, as well as *all active subnets* (IPv4 and IPv6) tied to the netname.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetinfo.UMnetinfo.get_vrfs
documentation:
Pulls data from the vrf table on netinfo. If you supply a name and/or rd, it filters only
for that vrf. Otherwise it returns all VRFs

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetinfo.UMnetinfo.get_special_acl
documentation: Looks for a special ACL assignment by netname

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetinfo.UMnetinfo.get_asns
documentation:
Pulls all the ASNs from the AUTONOMOUS_SYSTEM
table, optionally filtering by asname

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetinfo.UMnetinfo.get_dlzone_buildings
documentation:
given a dlzone shortname, returns a list of building numbers tied
to that zone.

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetinfo.UMnetinfo.get_dlzone_by_building
documentation:
given a seven-digit building number, return the name of the
distribution-zone to which it is assigned

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetinfo.UMnetinfo.get_user_groups
documentation: Looks up a user in netinfo and returns the groups they're in

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetinfo.UMnetinfo.get_network_admins
documentation:
Looks up the users and user groups that are tied to this network.
You can fiter by entity types (see UMNET_ONLINE.ENTITY_TYPE_CODE), these are:
  group, organization, person
You can also filter by relationship type (UMNET_ONLINE.ENTITEM_RELATIONSHIP_TYPE_CODE):
  Owner, Worker, Business, Admin, Security

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetinfo.UMnetinfo.get_device_admins
documentation:
Looks up the users and user groups that are tied to this device.
You can fiter by entity types (see UMNET_ONLINE.ENTITY_TYPE_CODE), these are:
  group, organization, person
You can also filter by relationship type (UMNET_ONLINE.ENTITEM_RELATIONSHIP_TYPE_CODE):
  Owner, Worker, Business, Admin, Security

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetinfo.UMnetinfo.get_arphist
documentation:
Given either a MAC address, IP address, or subnet, query UMNET.ARPHIST
and return the results.

If you don't care about history, set "no_hist"
If you don't care about which device the entries are routed on,
set "no_device"

Optionally limit the query by device and/or by interface
with the "device" and "interface" fields

methods.name: umnet_scripts.umnetinfo.UMnetinfo.get_prefix_lists
documentation:
Queries UMNET_ONLINE.PREFIX_LISTS for a list of prefix list names (as defined in the database).
If you don't provide a list of names it will return all of them
This class wraps helpful netinfo db queries in python.

modules.name: bin.equipdb_rancid_audit
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python

modules.name: bin.get_al_port_data
documentation: !venv/bin/python
